reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Verified: cache key construction (data_column.go:527-547) omits kzg_commitments, including only inclusion_proof[4*32] + header_root[32]. Two sidecars with identical proofs/headers but different commitments produce same cache key, causing second to bypass Merkle verification (line 368-369). Inclusion proof verification (peerdas.VerifyDataColumnSidecarInclusionProof, line 107) computes hash_tree_root(sidecar.KzgCommitments) as leaf, so skipping this allows accepting commitments not in block body. KZG proof verification is independent and only validates cells-match-commitments, not commitments-in-block. Attack confirmed exploitable. Severity downgraded from Critical to Medium per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json: Prysm share=31%, which exceeds Medium threshold (>5%) but not High threshold (>33%), capping single-client impact at Medium.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Spec SG-004 (verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof) requires Merkle proof links kzg_commitments to block body_root via is_valid_merkle_branch. Cache optimization violates this invariant by allowing unverified commitments to pass.
